name: Zero-Trust Architecture
def: Trusting no network location implicitly; verifying every request regardless of origin.
problem: Replace the broken "trusted internal network" perimeter model with continuous verification.
mechanism: Every request is authenticated, authorised and encrypted based on identity and device posture; "never trust, always verify", with micro-segmentation and least privilege.
failures: "Zero trust" in name but flat trusted network underneath
Identity provider as single point of failure
myths: "Zero trust is a product you buy" — it is an architecture and operating model
